expectations.text: Response identifies multiple domains (database, backend, frontend, responsive)
expectations.evidence: Domain Classification table explicitly maps to Database + RBAC, Backend / API, Data Tables, and Responsiveness domains with corresponding agents.

expectations.text: Response names specific agents or specializations for each domain
expectations.evidence: Names neon-master (database), backend-master (API), tanstack (data tables), shadcn-ui-designer (frontend), responsive-master (responsiveness), debug-master and qa-tester (verification), and plan-supervisor.

expectations.text: Response provides a sequential execution plan with ordering rationale
expectations.evidence: Section 'Sequential Invocation Order Rationale' explains why each agent runs in order: 'neon-master first -- The database schema defines the shape of all data... backend-master second -- tRPC procedures depend on the schema from neon-master...' etc.

expectations.text: Response includes verification/testing step at the end
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 includes debug-master ('Verify no TypeScript errors, Biome lint passes') and qa-tester ('Functional testing via agent-browser: CRUD operations work, sorting/filtering behaves correctly'). Post-Implementation section includes validation checklists.
